name: Mongo Santamaría
abstract: Ramón "Mongo" Santamaría Rodríguez (April 7, 1917 – February 1, 2003) was a Cuban percussionist and bandleader who spent most of his career in the United States. Primarily a conga drummer, Santamaría was a leading figure in the pachanga and boogaloo dance crazes of the 1960s.

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: Then began the friendship between us. At that time, Mongo was working for the postal service as I said before and I helped him to deliver the mail so he would finish his work early so that we could start rehearsing really early. That was how our friendship was and is until now, until now.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Vega
about_quotes.evidence: Thank goodness that Mongo Santamaria in 1987 gave me the opportunity to join his band
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Vega interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 20, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I really didn’t start writing a whole bunch until I played with Mongo Santamaria’s group and I started recording.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: I cannot find no original that Mongo ever did. I don't think -- his mind don't move that way. I don't think he got the capacity. I hate to say this, but let's put everything in a respectable place. That's my own opinion.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: And I played on all the salsa recordings in New York too. Fania Records, 1974, ’75, ’76. Willie Colon, Hector Lavoe, Ruben Blades, Johnny Pacheco, Mongo Santamaria, the Lebron brothers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Cascadu
about_quotes.evidence: Mongo Santamaria, Carlos Patato Valdez, Tito Puente, Celia Cruz, you know I mean all of the people, all of the people. Papo Pepin, I mean everybody came through there and played
about_quotes.doc_title: Cascadu interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019
